channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Results of the 2026 Golden Demon Painting Competition at AdeptiCon
item.description: The winners of the U.S. leg of the Golden Demon 2026 painting competition were recently announced at AdeptiCon in Milwaukee, Wisconsin. This prestigious event attracts talented painters from around th...

item.title: Olympio Metals reports high-grade gold intercepts at Quebec’s Bousquet project
item.description: Perth-based explorer Olympio Metals has reported outstanding gold drilling results from its Bousquet project in Quebec, Canada, sending its shares up by as much as 27 per cent in early trading. The st...

item.title: Fakhar Zaman faces ball-tampering charge in PSL clash with Karachi Kings
item.description: In a dramatic turn during the Pakistan Super League (PSL) match on Sunday, Lahore Qalandars batter Fakhar Zaman has been formally charged by the Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB) for ball-tampering. The in...

item.title: Government Moves to Dismiss Petition on State House Political Meetings
item.description: The Kenyan government has asked the High Court in Nairobi to throw out a petition that questions President William Ruto’s use of State House for political meetings. The case, filed by lawyer Lempaa Su...

item.title: Video shared online claiming to show FBI chief dancing is actually old and unrelated
item.description: On 29th March 2026, a video claiming to show FBI Director Kash Patel dancing went viral on various social media platforms including X, Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube. Multiple users from Ind...

item.title: Bendel Insurance fined N7 million as NPFL punishes security and conduct breaches; Coach Boboye suspended
item.description: For the Nigeria Premier Football League, discipline still dey crucial, and Bendel Insurance don show say dem fit fall short. For Matchday 32 against Plateau United on 28 March, security wahala and unr...

item.title: Amit Shah Declares India Free from Naxal Influence, Attributes Past Insurgency to Congress
item.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah on Monday officially declared India as Naxal-free in the Lok Sabha, marking a significant milestone in the government's fight against left-wing extremism. In an 84-minute...

item.title: Alberta seeks greater role in superior court judge appointments through constitutional changes
item.description: The Government of Alberta has announced its intention to pursue constitutional amendments that would grant the province a formal role in the appointment of superior court judges. Alberta officials arg...

item.title: One man pleads guilty, two plead not guilty in Saskatoon manslaughter case over 2024 shooting death
item.description: The trial related to the 2024 death of Jason Horse in Saskatoon began on Monday, bringing three accused before the court. During the proceedings, Tyrone Nepoose pleaded guilty to manslaughter, while h...

item.title: Nigerian Travel Agents Record 40% Fall in Ticket Sales Due to Middle East Crisis
item.description: The National Association of Nigerian Travel Agencies (NANTA) don dey cry say the ongoing Middle East conflict don seriously affect dem business, causing about 30 to 40 percent drop in ticket sales. Th...

item.title: Tesco in Orkney gives away 38,000 bananas after ordering error
item.description: A Tesco supermarket in Kirkwall, Orkney, has been distributing free bananas throughout the local community after staff accidentally ordered approximately 38,000 of them. The retailer had intended to r...

item.title: Koala’s ASX debut marks steady investor interest in furniture retailer
item.description: Koala, the Australian furniture retailer best known for its streamlined online shopping experience and fast delivery, has recorded a strong performance on its first day of trading on the Australian Se...

item.title: Implementation of Weekly Food Waste Collections Faces Delays in England
item.description: New regulations requiring weekly food waste collections for all households in England came into effect on 31 March, but a significant number of local councils are not fully prepared to comply. A BBC i...

item.title: Study Finds Exercise, Knee Braces, and Hydrotherapy Most Effective for Knee Osteoarthritis Pain
item.description: A large-scale analysis published in PLOS One has found that simple, non-drug therapies such as knee braces, hydrotherapy, and exercise are the most effective options for relieving pain caused by knee ...

item.title: Katie Couric Reflects on Her Late Husband's Illness and the Conversations She Regrets Avoiding
item.description: Katie Couric, former host of the 'Today' show, opened up about her greatest regret regarding her late husband, Jay Monahan, who passed away from colorectal cancer in 1998 at the age of 42. Couric shar...

item.title: Albert Korir handed five-year ban after admitting doping
item.description: Kenya’s own Albert Korir, who won the 2021 New York Marathon, has been slapped with a five-year ban after admitting to doping offences, the Athletics Integrity Unit (AIU) confirmed on Monday. The 31-y...

item.title: Titans Claim One-Day Cup After Challenging Season
item.description: The Titans emerged victorious in the One-Day Cup final against the Lions, securing a three-wicket win in the final over. After a challenging season that included losing their first four matches in the...

item.title: Actor Vijay Cancels Chennai Outreach Event Over Security Concerns Ahead Of Tamil Nadu Elections
item.description: In a sudden development ahead of the Tamil Nadu elections, popular actor and political figure Vijay has called off a planned outreach event in Chennai. The decision was reportedly due to concerns over...

item.title: Funke Akindele and Toyin Abraham clash quietly at Iyabo Ojo’s film premiere
item.description: At Iyabo Ojo’s movie premiere for *The Return of Arinzo*, a small but noticeable moment between Funke Akindele and Toyin Abraham has set social media on fire again. In a 20‑second clip that went viral...

item.title: Violent Protest Rocks South Africa Over Disputed Coronation Of Nigerian Man Claimed As King
item.description: Wahala don bust for South Africa as some community groups and traditional leaders for East London protest come turn bloody over the coronation of one Nigerian man, Solomon Ezika, wey claim say him be ...
